programme.title: Aur Pyar Ho Gaya
programme.desc: Two childhood friends plan to get their children married; little do they know that one friend's daughter is already in love with someone else.

programme.title: Baat Ban Gayi
programme.desc: Witness the extraordinary story of an immortal warrior.

programme.title: Banjo
programme.desc: Taraat, a banjoist hailing from a poor background, and his associates get the offer of a lifetime when they are approached by Christina, a musician from the US.

programme.title: Anjaana Anjaani
programme.desc: Two strangers who meet over a bridge, make a pact and travel together on a journey, get to know each other all that while!

programme.title: Purani Jeans
programme.desc: Purani Jeans is a film based on the belief that friendship is like a pair of old jeans -- the more you wear them the better it gets.

programme.title: Happy Bhag Jayegi
programme.desc: A girl decides to sprint out of her own wedding and has people from not one but two countries chasing after her.

programme.title: Salaam-E-Ishq
programme.desc: Six couples face problems in their lives when fate forces them to move away from each other. They must overcome these issues in order to make their love triumphant.

programme.title: Lajja
programme.desc: A pregnant woman witnesses atrocities committed on women around the country while on a run from her husband.

programme.title: Listen Amaya
programme.desc: Widowed mother, Leela, runs a cafe "Book a Coffee" where she meets a photographer Jayant and starts a relationship with him.

programme.title: Ra.one
programme.desc: Ra One is a Hindi Sci-Fi movie about Shekhar (Shah Rukh Khan) a gaming programmer, who develops an indestructible virtual character named Ra.One (Arjun Rampal). Soon, fiction turns to reality when Ra.One enters the real world to kill Shekhar & his son Prateek. Another virtual character G.One comes to rescue Prateek & Sonia (Kareena Kapoor). Watch Ra.One to know about the winner of final battle?

programme.title: Shamitabh
programme.desc: A mute, aspiring actor joins forces with a man who has a powerful voice. Together they take the film industry by storm, but will their egos get in the way?

programme.title: Subh Kushal Mangal
programme.desc: A group of friends, along with members of the Russian mafia, get stuck on a remote island off the coast of Goa when new drugs at a rave party are consumed by the guests, turning them into zombies.

programme.title: Table No. 21
programme.desc: At first, a couple is thrilled to participate in a game show with a huge cash prize, until they find out that the game isn't for fun, it's for survival.

programme.title: Devdas
programme.desc: Devdas is a tragic Hindi movie about Devdas (Shahrukh Khan) who returns home after studying for 10 years in London. Devdas shared a special bond with Paro (Aishwarya Rai) & wanted to marry her. But his family refuses Devdas's marriage to Paro, which shatters him and he turns to alcohol for solace. Watch Devdas to know whether he will come out of alcoholism & get the love of his life?

programme.title: Hijack
programme.desc: A plane is hijacked by six terrorists who demand the release of Rashid Omar, their accomplice, or else the hostages will be killed.

programme.title: Housefull
programme.desc: A mascot for bad luck, Aarush's life turns into comical chaos after his wife runs away on the honeymoon night.

programme.title: Kuch Kuch Locha Hai
programme.desc: A rich Gujarati businessman Praveenbhai lies about his marital status when he meets Shanaya, a beautiful Bollywood debutante.

programme.title: Main Krishna Hoon
programme.desc: Krishna, an orphan, lives in an orphanage. He feels unwanted as all his friends have found someone to adopt them. However, his sadness turns into happiness when Lord Krishna comes to his aid.

programme.title: Date Gone Wrong S1 Ep 1
programme.desc: She is a marketing consultant and he is a musician, they both hit it off. But little do they know that their connection goes way back.

programme.title: Date Gone Wrong S1 Ep 2
programme.desc: On a client meeting, a psychic healer is about to uncover the reasons behind Chef Vicky’s mysterious death.

programme.title: Date Gone Wrong S1 Ep 3
programme.desc: 'Murphy Bhagwaan is overlooking our lives and whatever bad is meant to happen, will happen nonetheless.' With the same principle, Anaya met her date but Murphy Bhagwaan had other plans for her.

programme.title: Date Gone Wrong S1 Ep 4
programme.desc: An Africa-return documentary filmmaker, he was everything she was looking for. But later she realizes that his preferences are too wild for her taste.

programme.title: Date Gone Wrong S1 Ep 5
programme.desc: Over an iced-tea and an Americano, he learns that she is a vlogger and she learns that he can’t stop checking out other women. In the world of online dating, what is considered as normal behaviour?

programme.title: Date Gone Wrong S1 Ep 6
programme.desc: With funny accents and poetry, a role-play date with an actor turned into a fun night, until he took it too far.

programme.title: Date Gone Wrong S1 Ep 7
programme.desc: They spent the whole day at the beach and at the end, he presented her with a gift. But instead of saying Thank you, she cursed him. What went wrong?

programme.title: Date Gone Wrong S1 Ep 8
programme.desc: 'Why are feminists so ugly?' By the end of 3 drinks, she was sure that they had nothing in common, but is it be possible that they had a connection?

programme.title: Date Gone Wrong S1 Ep 9
programme.desc: He was charming, she was drunk, they went back to her place but a series of questions led to a heated argument.

programme.title: Date Gone Wrong S1 Ep 10
programme.desc: Flowers, her favourite pasta and good wine, he had planned the perfect evening, but something went wrong at 10pm.

programme.title: Purani Jeans
programme.desc: Purani Jeans is a film based on the belief that friendship is like a pair of old jeans -- the more you wear them the better it gets.

programme.title: Shirin Farhad Ki Toh Nikal Padi
programme.desc: A middle aged Parsi couple find love at the most unexpected places and...time!

programme.title: Jeet Lengey Jahaan
programme.desc: Jeet Lengey Jahaan is about today's youngsters who are starving to achieve their goals in life. It shows how they get trapped into our corrupt system and face adverse circumstances and finally find themselves helpless and how they fight against it by their own ways.

programme.title: Lajja
programme.desc: A pregnant woman witnesses atrocities committed on women around the country while on a run from her husband.

programme.title: Daal Mein Kuch Kaala Hai
programme.desc: Daal Mein Kuch Kaala Hai is the humorous tale of a budding actress who must con a rich loser to become successful in Bollywood.

programme.title: Tevar
programme.desc: Caught in the middle of a lethal unrequited love, a kabaddi player tries to safeguard a young woman from an unwanted marriage with a dangerous faction leader.

programme.title: Phobia
programme.desc: Mehak, a talented artist, develops agoraphobia after a taxi driver assaults her. She moves to a new apartment and starts having visions, leading her to think that a malevolent force has possessed her.

programme.title: De Dana Dan
programme.desc: Nitin and Ram have been given an ultimatum by their rich girlfriends to either earn money or leave them alone. What follows then is a hilarious series of events!

programme.title: Desi Boyz
programme.desc: Two friends lose their jobs as the global recession hits, but their worlds really turn hilariously upside-down when they become male escorts to make ends meet.

programme.title: The Cliffhanger
programme.desc: In the aftermath of Riddhi's collapse, Zooni spearheads a desperate negotiation for better working conditions. As a seemingly balanced work environment emerges, the team struggles to deliver, and blurred lines between professional and personal lives begin to fray. Are these simply growing pains for Thoda Sukoon, or are fresh, explosive complications on the horizon?

programme.title: The Misfit
programme.desc: India’s fashion matriarch, Zahira, is making a bold move - bringing Zahiraan into the world of wearable luxury. But there’s a catch - neither she nor her team knows how to navigate this shift. Thus, comes in Ayesha - a rookie designer, a dreamer bursting with fresh ideas. But will she survive the cut-throat competition in fashion and at Zahiraan? Get ready for an intense drama, glamorous showdowns, and career-defining moments as the battle for success begins at Zahiraan.

programme.title: Time To Shine
programme.desc: Ayesha’s out-of-the-box ideas leave a mark on Zahira, but not everyone is impressed. As she tries to navigate the high-pressure world of fashion, her bold creativity ignites a war with her team. Amidst the chaos, an unexpected friendship with Sanchi gives Ayesha the confidence boost she desperately needs. But in an industry where talent isn’t enough, will this newfound support be her key to a major breakthrough?

programme.title: The Big Launch
programme.desc: The much-awaited Zahiraan launch is here, but disaster strikes! One unexpected error threatens to crumble Ayesha's hard-won validation from Zahira, and jeopardize both their careers. Meanwhile, Karan battles crippling self-doubt, wondering if he'll ever measure up to the perfection demanded by the high-stakes fashion world. Will their reputations survive?

programme.title: Perfect Shaadi
programme.desc: Aditya and Prajakta are all set to tie the knot! As they navigate the excitement and chaos of wedding planning, a surprise guest from Prajakta's past arrives, turning their perfect day upside down. Will love triumph over unexpected challenges?

programme.title: Basundi Ki Bhasudi
programme.desc: As Aditya and Prajakta celebrate their pre-wedding festivities, a shocking twist turns their world upside down! Will they handle the chaos together?

programme.title: Hum Tum Aur?
programme.desc: A perfect wedding, a hidden secret, and a love under threat. Will Aditya and Prajakta's love story survive the test of time?

programme.title: Bride Ka Best Man
programme.desc: As pre-wedding festivities unfold, Aditya stumbles upon a secret only Prajakta’s best friend knew. Can he handle the parts of her he’s just beginning to see?

programme.title: Imperfect Shaadi
programme.desc: The episode centers on the wedding of the lead couple, where everything seemingly goes wrong. However, instead of letting the chaos ruin the day, the couple realizes that the "imperfections" of their wedding reflect the reality of their relationship. They choose to embrace the mess, focusing on their commitment to each other.

programme.title: Infection
programme.desc: The series introduces Nishant, Sakshi, and Huma as they begin their MBBS journey, adjusting to the demanding environment of medical college and forming new friendships.

programme.title: Diagnosis
programme.desc: A month into their studies, the trio struggles to balance an overwhelming syllabus, challenging practicals, and the desire to enjoy college life. An unexpected revelation about Nishant changes the group's dynamics.

programme.title: Complications
programme.desc: Midterm results bring surprises, prompting celebrations that uncover deeper issues the friends must confront together.

programme.title: Surgery
programme.desc: Huma faces a crisis of confidence, seeking unconventional advice to regain her footing. A heartbreaking event forces the friends to reevaluate their priorities and the strength of their bond.

programme.title: Recovery
programme.desc: As final exams approach, Huma contemplates unethical shortcuts, leading to revelations that challenge perceptions and test the integrity of their friendship.

programme.title: Honeymoon Period
programme.desc: The series introduces Nishant, Sakshi, and Huma as they begin their MBBS journey, adjusting to the demanding environment of medical college and forming new friendships.

programme.title: Donning
programme.desc: A month into their studies, the trio struggles to balance an overwhelming syllabus, challenging practicals, and the desire to enjoy college life. An unexpected revelation about Nishant changes the group's dynamics.

programme.title: Patient Zero
programme.desc: Midterm results bring surprises, prompting celebrations that uncover deeper issues the friends must confront together.

programme.title: Lockdown
programme.desc: Huma faces a crisis of confidence, seeking unconventional advice to regain her footing. A heartbreaking event forces the friends to reevaluate their priorities and the strength of their bond.

programme.title: Critical Condition
programme.desc: As final exams approach, Huma contemplates unethical shortcuts, leading to revelations that challenge perceptions and test the integrity of their friendship.

programme.title: Fomo
programme.desc: Dhruv and Kavya plan a lazy Sunday but feel the pressure of missing out when they see their friends enjoying exciting activities on social media. This leads them to seek ways to spice up their day.

programme.title: Have A Nice Day
programme.desc: A morning rush to work results in a tussle between Dhruv and Kavya, spoiling their moods. Throughout the day, they attempt to make amends, highlighting the irony and sweetness of their relationship.

programme.title: Good Night
programme.desc: A sleepless night due to mosquitoes leads to the discovery that Kavya's ex-boyfriend is getting married, prompting a long conversation about their past relationships.

programme.title: Thank You!
programme.desc: Plans for a dinner at an Iranian restaurant get canceled, leading Dhruv and Kavya to attend a friend's house party. The night evolves into an unexpected adventure, ending at a local fair.

programme.title: Here We Go!
programme.desc: During a visit to a vineyard, Dhruv and Kavya open up about their insecurities and future aspirations, leading to deeper conversations about life and their relationship.

programme.title: Masterji
programme.desc: Kishore, an orphan has only one dream, to join the IndianArmy. When he gets thrown out of his orphanage, Masterji, an ex-army officer who left the army and is now working as a tailor, reluctantly adopts him. The khadoos Masterji and Kishore have only two things in common, their dream to serve the nation and their lonely lives. Will Masterji help Kishore achieve his own unfulfilled dreams to become a soldier, and will Kishore find a father in Masterji?

programme.title: Jaldi Lautna, Captain
programme.desc: Captain Vikrant Sahay and his wife Vibha had a beautiful love story. But Vikrant’s sudden death in battle leaves the otherwise happy-go-lucky Vibha completely shocked. Unable to deal with Vikrant’s death, Vibha decides that the best way to honor him is to fulfill his dream, by joining the army herself, much against her family’s wishes. Will Vibha succeed and make her late husband, her family, and the country proud?

programme.title: Shooter Jawan
programme.desc: Tribhuvan, a dedicated Indian Army Officer comes across a young sweeper Tinku, who is a gifted shooter. Tribhuvan knows Tinku has a rare talent, and wants to enroll him in the shooting academy and then the Indian Army. However, owing to Tinku’s caste, his father is afraid of society’s taunts and is completely against this career choice. Will Tinku have the courage to fight back and rise above ‘log kya kahenge’?

programme.title: Sarhad Ke Baad Bhi
programme.desc: Colonel Balram, a retired Indian Army Officer, returns home after several years of service, only to find himself completely disconnected from his son Aman. While Balram is failing at mending his relationship with Aman, he is also silently struggling with mental health issues, because of his days at war. Will Aman ever understand his father’s sacrifices for the country or will it be too late before he realises just how much his father loves him?

programme.title: Veera
programme.desc: Veera’s martyred father’s last wish was for her to join the Indian armed forces. But Veera is not for it. The bravery and patriotism that the country remembers and celebrates him for, are the same things that took Veera’s father away from her. She’s been carrying this anger and hurt in her heart for too long, until she discovers a hidden letter.

programme.title: Bachelorhood
programme.desc: Viraj and Arjun have been best friends since childhood. While Viraj loves his bachelor life and wants Arjun to move in as his flatmate, Arjun has other plans of ‘settling down’. What happens when this bachelor reveals he wants to be ‘unbachelored?’

programme.title: Kabab Mein Haddi
programme.desc: The gang gets together to celebrate Arjun’s big news, but Viraj is concerned whether the dynamics of the group will change with its new addition. Will Khushi win over the gang and get Viraj's stamp of approval?

programme.title: (un)happy Birthday
programme.desc: Its Arjun’s birthday, and Khushi steals the limelight with her unconventional celebration party. Tensions at the party rise when Viraj confronts Arjun about his change in behaviour since he started dating Khushi. Will Arjun pick pyaar or dosti?

programme.title: Mann Ki Rant
programme.desc: The group tries to resolve tensions between Viraj and Arjun, but they both resist. As Viraj rants to Zara and Kaka about his friendships and relationships, he realises more about his true self.

programme.title: Yaar Ki Shaadi
programme.desc: The wedding day is here! Will Viraj stand by Arjun on the most important day of his life, and will he admit his true feelings to a special someone?

programme.title: Conflict Of Interest
programme.desc: Shagun wants to keep the Filmy Weddings business floating and for that she’s willing to go to any lengths. Even if it means shooting a wedding film for Adhira’s ex-boyfriend – Akhil. Adhira is furious and tries her best to sabotage the entire project. Unfortunately, her one big hurdle is Dad, who has taken quite a fondness for Akhil. Check out the episode and find out what happens next!

programme.title: Internal Politics
programme.desc: Adhira and Shagun are in a disagreement about whom to select for a wedding film promo: a boring aunt from the bride's side or a lively drunk aunt from the groom's family. Things take a turn for the complicated when the parents also weigh in with their respective biases.

programme.title: Poaching The Talent
programme.desc: Vishnu, the Agarwal's househelp cum man friday is desired by the sisters as an office boy to help them sort out their business affairs. The parents are naturally opposed to the idea. Thus begins another standoff in the Agarwal household - and Vishnu's position in the house is at stake. Who will finally lay claim over Vishnu's priceless services?

programme.title: Business Secrets
programme.desc: In a footage from a wedding they just filmed, the sisters find a drunken confession from the bride's best friend. It turns out she is the groom's ex - and he hasn't told the bride to be. Caught in the moral dilemma of choosing to tell the bride or not, the sisters find themselves on other ends of the argument. Meanwhile, it is revealed that Adhira's father might have a similar secret hidden from everyone. Will the sisters do the morally right thing or the financially right thing? And what is the father's bizarre obsession with knowing who the groom's mom is?

programme.title: Breach Of Contract
programme.desc: Chasing a competitive business pitch to work for a coveted fashion firm, the sisters are again at different sides of a dilemma. The commitment to the new opportunity would be for a minimum two years, which Adhira is reluctant to make at the age of 25. Whereas Shagun, having already quit her high paying corporate job to join this business, sees no harm in seizing the opportunity with open arms. As their argument turns to a fight, we are left wondering if this is in fact the fight to end all fights. Will the sisters come to the same page and work for the pitch? Or will Happily Hitched disband for good?

programme.title: Arranged Family
programme.desc: Nikhil Solanki arrives at his in-laws' home, where a series of mishaps culminate in a significant blunder that upsets his father-in-law, Prakash Sharma. Both struggle to address the situation openly.

programme.title: The Mad House
programme.desc: Prakash avoids acknowledging that Nikhil damaged his car, instead blaming neighborhood children. Nikhil's attempts to rectify the situation lead to further complications.

programme.title: Conditions Apply
programme.desc: During Sudha and Prakash's anniversary celebration, relatives pose uncomfortable questions, challenging Nikhil's patience and composure.

programme.title: Stuck In The Middle
programme.desc: A disagreement between Anita and her parents results in a cold war, leaving Nikhil caught between his wife and in-laws.

programme.title: Home
programme.desc: Nikhil expresses his feelings of being an outsider within the family, prompting an open discussion aimed at resolving underlying issues.

programme.title: Welcome Home
programme.desc: A few months after moving to Mumbai, Nikhil (Veer Rajwant Singh) and Anita are finally settled. The peace is interrupted by the arrival of Nikhil's parents, Kiran (Shishir Sharma) and Vandana (Renuka Shahane), for a short visit. Anita, ever the perfectionist, is anxious to be the "perfect daughter-in-law," but her efforts are met with a series of awkward and hilarious hurdles.

programme.title: Surprise, Surprise!
programme.desc: The episode picks up with the fallout of Nikhil’s father, Kiran (Shishir Sharma), going missing during his walk in the previous episode. While he eventually returns, the tension doesn't dissipate. The "surprise" of the title is twofold: the unexpected arrival of Nikhil’s sister, Shreya (Kriti Vij), and the unsettling realization that something is physically or mentally "off" with Kiran.

programme.title: Spilling The Beans
programme.desc: The entire family—Nikhil (Veer Rajwant Singh), Anita (Eisha Chopra), Kiran (Shishir Sharma), Vandana (Renuka Shahane), and Shreya (Kriti Vij)—piles into a car to attend a relative's 60th birthday celebration. What was meant to be a joyful getaway becomes a pressure cooker for the long-standing resentment between the siblings.

programme.title: Something's Missing
programme.desc: The title is a poignant double entendre: it refers to a missing set of rings at the family function, but more deeply, it refers to the "missing" pieces of Kiran’s (Shishir Sharma) memory. The facade of a "normal family vacation" finally crumbles, forcing the Solankis to stop pretending.

programme.title: House Of Cards
programme.desc: The title "House of Cards" refers to the fragile "perfect family" image the Solankis have tried to maintain. After the emotional explosion regarding Kiran’s (Shishir Sharma) health and Shreya’s (Kriti Vij) marriage, the "house" has finally collapsed—but in its place, something more honest is being built.

programme.title: Home
programme.desc: In the Season 2 finale, "Home," Anita and Nikhil prepare to move into their own place. The episode resolves the season's friction between Anita and her mother-in-law, replacing awkward formalities with genuine mutual respect. As they move out, the story concludes that "home" isn't about physical space, but the emotional bridge built between two different generations.

programme.title: Circle Of Life
programme.desc: The title "Circle of Life" refers to the poignant role reversal where the children now become the guardians of their parents, mirroring the care they once received.

programme.title: Not Kidding
programme.desc: The episode centers on a visit from Anita’s boisterous, unfiltered parents—Akshata (Deepika Amin) and Kiran Sharma (Shishir Sharma). The quiet, disciplined, and currently somber Solanki household is suddenly upended by the high-energy Sharmas. The primary conflict arises when the two sets of parents have a "polite clash" over how to care for the ailing Kiran Solanki and, more pivotally, the couple's future plans regarding children.

programme.title: Here We Go Again
programme.desc: With the house now fully "merged," Anita (Eisha Chopra) and Nikhil (Veer Rajwant Singh) decide to throw a surprise anniversary party for the "Folks." While their intentions are pure, the episode explores how a "celebration" can quickly become a source of stress when dealing with a chronic illness and interfering relatives.

programme.title: Zor Se Bolo
programme.desc: The episode revolves around a chaotic Sunday at the Solanki household. The central plot involves Kiran Solanki (Shishir Sharma) needing a hearing aid, but it quickly spirals into a larger commentary on how the family members are "deaf" to each other's actual needs and desires.

programme.title: That's All Folks
programme.desc: The finale moves away from the day-to-day bickering and centers on a quiet realization: Nikhil (Veer Rajwant Singh) and Anita (Eisha Chopra) have finally stopped "adjusting" and have started living. The episode focuses on the family accepting their "New Normal" while acknowledging that the road ahead with Kiran’s (Shishir Sharma) health will be challenging but shared.

programme.title: Truth Bombs
programme.desc: The episode begins with Nikhil (Veer Rajwant Singh) and Anita (Esha Chopra) gathering the family for a "serious talk." After months of private deliberation, they drop the ultimate truth bomb: they have decided to adopt a child instead of pursuing a biological pregnancy.

programme.title: The Damaad Test
programme.desc: The episode centers on the Home Study, a critical step in the Indian adoption process where a government social worker visits the family to determine if they are fit to raise a child. However, the title "The Damaad Test" (The Son-in-law Test) refers to the parallel pressure Nikhil (Veer Rajwant Singh) feels to prove he is a "good son" while also being a "good son-in-law" to Anita’s (Eisha Chopra) visiting parents.

programme.title: Ladkiwale Vs. Ladkewale
programme.desc: While Anita (Eisha Chopra) and Nikhil (Veer Rajwant Singh) are stuck in the agonizing "waiting period" of the CARA process, the tension shifts to the two sets of parents. The episode highlights that even when everyone agrees on the goal (the grandchild), their different ways of expressing love and anxiety can lead to a hilarious yet deep-seated cultural clash.

programme.title: Are We Ready?
programme.desc: The wait is over: Anita and Nikhil are matched with a child. However, instead of the pure euphoria they expected, they are hit with a wave of intense anxiety. The title "Are We Ready?" reflects the internal crisis the couple faces as they realize their lives are about to change irrevocably—not in nine months, but in a matter of days.

programme.title: What The Future?
programme.desc: The finale begins with the arrival of the moment Anita (Eisha Chopra) and Nikhil (Veer Rajwant Singh) have spent the entire season preparing for: bringing their adopted daughter home. The episode isn't just about the new arrival, but about how each family member adapts to their "What The Future" role.

programme.title: Comfort Zone
programme.desc: Shaurya struggles with getting recognition for his efforts at work while Sanya debates shifting out of her home. Both of them help each other step out of their comfort zones and grow

programme.title: Time Off
programme.desc: As Shaurya and Sanya shift in together, Sanya takes a gamble at work which impacts both of their lives.

programme.title: Emotion Or Logic
programme.desc: As Shaurya and Sanya deal with the realization of having fallen for each other, they have to prioritize what is more important - emotions or logic?

programme.title: New Roles
programme.desc: Having finally admitted their feelings to each other, Shaurya and Sanya's honeymoon weekend is over and they must get back to reality. With Shaurya up for a promotion and Sanya doubtful about her growth in this company, what is this new couple in for?

programme.title: Milestones
programme.desc: With appraisals around the corner, Shaurya and Sanya brace themselves for the unexpected. Eventually, Shaurya gets his first real lesson in team management and Sanya makes a crucial decision to help her get what she wants.

programme.title: Month-Iversary
programme.desc: To mark 3 months of being together, Shaurya convinces Sanya to organise something for their "monthiversary." However, things begin to get complicated as Shaurya finds himself working directly with Sanya - for the first time since they started dating.

programme.title: Choices
programme.desc: Shaurya eventually ends up at Akhil’s party but to his surprise does not feel like he belongs there. Meanwhile, Ratna invites Sanya to have a drink with her. Sanya still upset about Shaurya lying to her begins to feel better with Ratna and her friends. To her surprise Shaurya shows up there as well to save their monthi-versary. However, things seem to go in the opposite direction soon.

programme.title: Time Off
programme.desc: Shaurya’s presentation does not go as planned and he ends up overpromising samples to the client on a three day deadline. This leads to the team having to work round the clock. Meanwhile, Sanya makes a plan to take a break from work and convinces Shaurya to do go somewhere together. Both Shaurya and Sanya finally spend some quality time with each other but things take a turn when Shaurya gets a call from Vinod.

programme.title: Last Shot
programme.desc: Sanya decides to move back to her room. Shaurya isn’t happy about this. To make things worse Akhil is hard on him for overpromising and almost losing the client. Akhil demands that the team fixes the situation and gets them another chance.

programme.title: Broke Af
programme.desc: The episode sets the stage for the series: Ray and Nikhat are best friends and roommates who have just started their journey into independent adulthood. The central conflict is the classic "End of the Month" crisis—where your bank account is empty, but your social and survival needs are not.

programme.title: Mommy Knows Best
programme.desc: The episode centers on a surprise visit from Nikhat’s mother, which triggers a high-speed cleaning operation. For any young person living away from home, the "parental visit" is a performance of maturity, and Ray and Nikhat are determined to win an Oscar for "Most Responsible Adults."

programme.title: Friends Ya Followers
programme.desc: The episode explores the gap between digital perception and reality. Ray and Nikhat find themselves caught in a cycle of comparing their "boring" lives to the filtered, high-glamour lives of their peers on Instagram.

programme.title: On My Way
programme.desc: The episode follows a high-stakes day for both girls. Nikhat (Aisha Ahmed) is trying to climb the corporate ladder and has a critical presentation, while Ray (Yashaswini Dayama) is dealing with the thrill and anxiety of a potential romantic connection. The title reflects the constant "hustle" and the physical and metaphorical state of being "in transit" in life.

programme.title: Now And Then
programme.desc: The episode is framed around a significant milestone: Nikhat’s (Aisha Ahmed) birthday. While birthdays are usually for celebrating, they often trigger a "Quarter-Life Crisis" where we compare our current reality to the versions of ourselves we imagined "then."

programme.title: Just Kiddin
programme.desc: The episode revolves around the nightmare of moving houses in Mumbai. Ray and Nikhat are excited about their new "adult" space, but the transition from the old house to the new one becomes a comedy of errors that tests their patience and their friendship.

programme.title: Work It Out
programme.desc: The episode explores the "Work-Life Balance" myth. Nikhat (Aisha Ahmed), now in a senior role with a fancy new apartment to pay for, is buried under deadlines, while Ray (Yashaswini Dayama) is trying to figure out how to be a "productive" freelancer without the structure of an office.

programme.title: Me Vs Us
programme.desc: The episode centers on the loss of personal space. As Ray (Yashaswini Dayama) and Nikhat (Aisha Ahmed) spend more time in their new apartment, the "honeymoon phase" of moving in ends. The title reflects the struggle to maintain one’s own identity ("Me") while being part of a co-dependent duo ("Us").

programme.title: Not Cool
programme.desc: The episode centers on the arrival of Ray’s (Yashaswini Dayama) old school friend. Ray is excited to show off her "cool" Mumbai life, her "cool" roommate Nikhat (Aisha Ahmed), and her "cool" new apartment. However, the reunion doesn't go as planned, leading to a major case of "Social Comparison Anxiety."

programme.title: Challenge Accepted
programme.desc: Everything that could go wrong, does. The episode is a "comedy of errors" that quickly turns into an emotional crucible. As the girls juggle professional disasters and personal health scares, they are forced to answer the question: Are we just friends because it’s convenient, or are we family?

programme.title: Pawri Ho Rahi Hai
programme.desc: The episode picks up with Ray (Yashaswini Dayama) and Nikhat (Aisha Ahmed) feeling the absolute peak of urban exhaustion. Between Nikhat’s soul-crushing corporate deadlines and Ray’s chaotic freelance schedule, they realize they are running on fumes. To blow off some steam before their planned trip home, they decide to have one last "epic" Mumbai night.

programme.title: Rat Trap
programme.desc: The episode follows Ray (Yashaswini Dayama) and Nikhat (Aisha Ahmed) as they settle into their respective family homes. The initial "pampering phase" (where parents cook your favorite meals and do your laundry) ends abruptly, replaced by the suffocating routine and expectations they once ran away from.

programme.title: Old Dots, New Lines
programme.desc: While still in their respective hometowns, Ray (Yashaswini Dayama) and Nikhat (Aisha Ahmed) encounter people from their past. The episode highlights the awkward friction that occurs when you realize your old friends have either stayed exactly the same or changed in ways you can no longer relate to.

programme.title: Sisterhood
programme.desc: The episode explores the complex, multi-generational layers of female relationships. It’s not just about the friendship between the two leads, but about the "sisterhood" they share with their mothers and the realization that their mothers were once young, ambitious, and confused women too.

programme.title: Out Of Office
programme.desc: The title "Out Of Office" is a play on their professional status, but it more accurately reflects their mental state. They’ve been "out of office" from their Mumbai lives for long enough to gain a new perspective. The episode focuses on the inevitable departure and the emotional weight of saying goodbye—this time with the maturity of adults who know exactly what they are going back to.

programme.title: Nero The Hero
programme.desc: The episode opens with a camera crew following Neerav Kapoor (Nero) as he prepares for his day. Within the first five minutes, it becomes clear that Nero doesn't just want to be an actor; he believes he is already a legendary craftsman who simply hasn't been "discovered" yet.

programme.title: The Tables Turn
programme.desc: The episode explores the concept of "Networking." Nero believes that his talent is so undeniable that if he can just get in front of the right person, the "tables will turn," and he will go from a nobody to a superstar. This leads him to infiltrate a high-profile industry event where he is, unsurprisingly, not fit.

programme.title: The Agony Aunt
programme.desc: Nero realizes that being a great actor isn't just about performing; it’s about observation and mentorship. He decides to take it upon himself to solve the "emotional blocks" of everyone around him, believing that his vast experience in playing fictional characters makes him an expert in real-world psychology.

programme.title: Superheroes
programme.desc: Nero becomes obsessed with the idea that he is the perfect fit for a "desi" superhero franchise. He doesn't wait for a script or a casting call; he decides to live the life of a superhero to "absorb the essence" of the character. This leads to some of the most cringeworthy "public" moments in the entire series.

programme.title: Getting Functional
programme.desc: Nero becomes convinced that the secret to becoming a superstar lies in "Physical Functionality." He decides that his body needs to be a fine-tuned instrument capable of any physical feat, leading him to join a CrossFit-style gym. However, in true Nero fashion, he doesn't just work out—he turns the gym into a stage for his "performative athleticism."

programme.title: In-House Talent
programme.desc: Nero is faced with a harsh reality: his bank account is empty, and his "blockbuster" hasn't arrived. He decides to grace a small-scale, in-house corporate production with his "legendary" presence. He views this as a charitable act—a superstar helping out the common folk—but the corporate team views him as a total nightmare.

programme.title: Reflections Of Blood
programme.desc: Nero decides that the mainstream industry is too "small-minded" to understand his vision. He takes on the role of Writer, Director, and Lead Actor to produce an avant-garde short film titled Reflections of Blood.

programme.title: Equal Rights
programme.desc: Nero realizes that "socially conscious" cinema is the current trend in the industry. He decides that to be a true "Hero," he needs to stand up for the marginalized. However, because it's Nero, he manages to turn a fight for "Equal Rights" into a self-centered campaign for "Actor’s Rights."

programme.title: High Is Well That Ends Well
programme.desc: The title is a classic Nero-ism—a mangled version of "All's Well That Ends Well," infused with his obsession with "vibrations" and high-frequency living. In this finale, Nero is faced with the ultimate test: a real, high-stakes opportunity that requires him to be... normal.

programme.title: All's Well That Ends Well
programme.desc: The documentary crew follows a rare moment of "crisis" in Nero’s life. His landlord has had enough of the "acting workshops" (noise) and unpaid dues. Nero, however, refuses to see this as a tragedy; he views it as the "Second Act" of his biopic where the hero hits rock bottom before the inevitable rise.

programme.title: Respawn
programme.desc: Arun (Vishal Vashishtha) is a former professional gamer, once known as "Flickshot," who left his passion to work a monotonous corporate job and support his family. When he sees his former teammate, Shekhar, succeeding on a massive stage, his suppressed passion resurfaces. Despite his father’s disapproval and the general stigma surrounding gaming as a career, Arun decides to quit his job and attempt a comeback.

programme.title: A New Beginning
programme.desc: Arun realizes he can’t win alone and begins the hunt for a new team. The episode focuses on the recruitment of a "misfit" squad: Prachi: A badminton player struggling with an injury. Jeetu: A delivery boy with natural, lightning-fast reflexes. Adil: A young, tech-savvy enthusiast who acts as the team's initial bridge. Raunak: A volatile but highly skilled "fragger." The task is harder than Arun anticipated, as he has to convince these individuals (and their skeptical families) that they have what it takes to be pros.99999999999

programme.title: Noobde (noobs)
programme.desc: The team has assembled, but the chemistry is non-existent. Raunak’s ego clashes with the other members, and the group struggles to put their personal issues aside during practice. Arun has to step up not just as a player, but as a coach, to teach them that in Esports, individual brilliance is useless without team synergy. This episode highlights the "grind" of professional gaming and the steep learning curve.

programme.title: Under Pressure
programme.desc: With the tournament only a week away, the pressure reaches a boiling point. Arun’s father discovers he quit his job, leading to a major confrontation. Meanwhile, the team faces a crushing defeat in a practice match against established professionals, leaving their confidence shattered. However, seeing Arun's dedication eventually inspires his father to have a change of heart, and the team finally begins to gel as a unit.

programme.title: Rise Of The Underdogs
programme.desc: The season finale takes place at the Indian Pro Gaming League. Team Clutch enters as the ultimate underdogs. After a shaky start and a brief internal conflict where Raunak almost walks out, the team reunites for a final stand. They reach a thrilling tie-breaker round where they must prove their worth. The series ends with the team securing a victory, using their prize money to chase their individual dreams while planning to build a permanent gaming headquarters.

programme.title: Rangbaaji (the Carefree Persona)
programme.desc: We are introduced to Raviranjan, a small-town guy who balances life as a part-time YouTuber with his status as a "full-time" aspirant. While he acts confident (his rangbaaji), the episode reveals the underlying pressure he faces. He isn’t just seeking a job; he’s seeking the social status required to marry Jyoti. The episode sets up the stakes: it's a battle between his dreams and the harsh deadlines of the competitive exam world.

programme.title: Parishram (the Effort)
programme.desc: The reality check hits hard when the OSC results are announced and Ravi fails his first attempt. He faces taunts from his family and the crushing weight of societal judgment. Encouraged by Jyoti, who provides both emotional support and a strategic plan, Ravi moves from being a "casual" student to a "hardcore" aspirant. This episode highlights the physical and mental "grind" involved in the preparation.

programme.title: Avroh (the Descent)
programme.desc: As the wait for the next exam cycle continues, the "high" of hard work starts to fade into a "low" (Avroh means descent). Financial struggles, family tensions, and unexpected "villains" in the form of societal gatekeepers threaten to derail him. Ravi is forced to make difficult choices between his relationship with Jyoti and his focus on the exam, as the cracks in his mental health begin to show.

programme.title: Prateeksha (the Finale The Wait)
programme.desc: The finale focuses on the most agonizing part of the process: the wait. Ravi faces his final hurdles and a major internal crisis of self-doubt. The episode serves as a powerful commentary on the Indian examination system, exploring whether a job title truly defines a person's worth. It concludes Raviranjan's journey by showing how he manages to reclaim his self-respect (Swabhiman), regardless of the exam result.

programme.title: Humse Na Ho Paayega
programme.desc: The episode sets the stage for the ultimate "Generation Gap" social experiment. Akash (Rohan Shah) and Pooja (Aditi Myakal) are a modern married couple living in a spacious Mumbai apartment. However, their lifestyle is outpacing their income, leading them to a radical decision: renting out their spare rooms to bachelors.

programme.title: Khan-Daan
programme.desc: After the initial adjustment period of the pilot, the household faces its first major logistical crisis—Food. The episode explores the chaos of shared groceries, differing diets, and the "disaster" that is a communal refrigerator.

programme.title: Safai-Squad
programme.desc: After weeks of living together, the apartment has reached a state of "critical mess." Aashna (Kriti Vij), who takes pride in a clean and aesthetic home, finally snaps. The episode follows the couple's attempt to militarize the house cleaning process and the bachelors' hilariously lazy resistance.

programme.title: Family Ban Gayi
programme.desc: The episode begins with a sense of impending change. After weeks of chaos, the experimental living arrangement—where a married couple (Akash and Pooja) shares their home with three bachelors (Rohit, Mohit, and Vishi)—is put to the test.

programme.title: Hotel California
programme.desc: Maya, a Gen Z UX designer, joins Thoda Sukoon — a mental health startup with strict office hours, long workdays, and zero breathing space. Expecting flexibility and support, she instead finds herself caught in a whirlwind of deadlines and old-school hustle. As pressure mounts ahead of a major product launch and funding round, Maya connects with her burnt-out teammates. But when it comes to challenging the system — who’s willing to speak up?

programme.title: The Launch
programme.desc: Tight deadlines. Zero chill. And one very fed-up Maya. Maya’s ideas get ghosted, her dedication’s under the scanner, and the team? Busy minding their own burnout. Just when she finds a bit of empathy, things spiral again. Sleep is a myth, pressure is real, and Maya’s had enough. But when push comes to shove… does she stay, or finally hit CTRL+ALT+ESC?

programme.title: All Is Well
programme.desc: Maya’s impulsive decision leaves her awkward around Chirag, who doesn’t miss a chance to tease her. As they open up about their generational views on work and life, Maya reaffirms her belief in mental health and Thoda Sukoon’s mission. But just as things start looking up, a bold move in a high-stakes meeting sparks serious tension with the higher-ups. Can Maya recover when her values put her at odds with the very system she’s trying to change?

programme.title: The Breakdown
programme.desc: While the team scrambles to fix minor app bugs, the real issues at Thoda Sukoon are swept under the rug — until it all explodes. An employee collapses from overwork, triggering a full-blown social media backlash. As tensions rise, Maya and Chirag are forced to face unresolved conflict, and the team must ask: how far is too far? Can this crisis spark real change in a toxic work culture, or is it already too late?

programme.title: The Cliffhanger
programme.desc: In the aftermath of Riddhi's collapse, Zooni spearheads a desperate negotiation for better working conditions. As a seemingly balanced work environment emerges, the team struggles to deliver, and blurred lines between professional and personal lives begin to fray. Are these simply growing pains for Thoda Sukoon, or are fresh, explosive complications on the horizon?

programme.title: The Misfit
programme.desc: India’s fashion matriarch, Zahira, is making a bold move - bringing Zahiraan into the world of wearable luxury. But there’s a catch - neither she nor her team knows how to navigate this shift. Thus, comes in Ayesha - a rookie designer, a dreamer bursting with fresh ideas. But will she survive the cut-throat competition in fashion and at Zahiraan? Get ready for an intense drama, glamorous showdowns, and career-defining moments as the battle for success begins at Zahiraan.

programme.title: Time To Shine
programme.desc: Ayesha’s out-of-the-box ideas leave a mark on Zahira, but not everyone is impressed. As she tries to navigate the high-pressure world of fashion, her bold creativity ignites a war with her team. Amidst the chaos, an unexpected friendship with Sanchi gives Ayesha the confidence boost she desperately needs. But in an industry where talent isn’t enough, will this newfound support be her key to a major breakthrough?

programme.title: The Big Launch
programme.desc: The much-awaited Zahiraan launch is here, but disaster strikes! One unexpected error threatens to crumble Ayesha's hard-won validation from Zahira, and jeopardize both their careers. Meanwhile, Karan battles crippling self-doubt, wondering if he'll ever measure up to the perfection demanded by the high-stakes fashion world. Will their reputations survive?

programme.title: Perfect Shaadi
programme.desc: Aditya and Prajakta are all set to tie the knot! As they navigate the excitement and chaos of wedding planning, a surprise guest from Prajakta's past arrives, turning their perfect day upside down. Will love triumph over unexpected challenges?

programme.title: Basundi Ki Bhasudi
programme.desc: As Aditya and Prajakta celebrate their pre-wedding festivities, a shocking twist turns their world upside down! Will they handle the chaos together?

programme.title: Hum Tum Aur?
programme.desc: A perfect wedding, a hidden secret, and a love under threat. Will Aditya and Prajakta's love story survive the test of time?

programme.title: Bride Ka Best Man
programme.desc: As pre-wedding festivities unfold, Aditya stumbles upon a secret only Prajakta’s best friend knew. Can he handle the parts of her he’s just beginning to see?

programme.title: Imperfect Shaadi
programme.desc: The episode centers on the wedding of the lead couple, where everything seemingly goes wrong. However, instead of letting the chaos ruin the day, the couple realizes that the "imperfections" of their wedding reflect the reality of their relationship. They choose to embrace the mess, focusing on their commitment to each other.

programme.title: Infection
programme.desc: The series introduces Nishant, Sakshi, and Huma as they begin their MBBS journey, adjusting to the demanding environment of medical college and forming new friendships.

programme.title: Diagnosis
programme.desc: A month into their studies, the trio struggles to balance an overwhelming syllabus, challenging practicals, and the desire to enjoy college life. An unexpected revelation about Nishant changes the group's dynamics.

programme.title: Complications
programme.desc: Midterm results bring surprises, prompting celebrations that uncover deeper issues the friends must confront together.

programme.title: Surgery
programme.desc: Huma faces a crisis of confidence, seeking unconventional advice to regain her footing. A heartbreaking event forces the friends to reevaluate their priorities and the strength of their bond.

programme.title: Recovery
programme.desc: As final exams approach, Huma contemplates unethical shortcuts, leading to revelations that challenge perceptions and test the integrity of their friendship.

programme.title: Honeymoon Period
programme.desc: The series introduces Nishant, Sakshi, and Huma as they begin their MBBS journey, adjusting to the demanding environment of medical college and forming new friendships.

programme.title: Donning
programme.desc: A month into their studies, the trio struggles to balance an overwhelming syllabus, challenging practicals, and the desire to enjoy college life. An unexpected revelation about Nishant changes the group's dynamics.

programme.title: Patient Zero
programme.desc: Midterm results bring surprises, prompting celebrations that uncover deeper issues the friends must confront together.

programme.title: Lockdown
programme.desc: Huma faces a crisis of confidence, seeking unconventional advice to regain her footing. A heartbreaking event forces the friends to reevaluate their priorities and the strength of their bond.

programme.title: Critical Condition
programme.desc: As final exams approach, Huma contemplates unethical shortcuts, leading to revelations that challenge perceptions and test the integrity of their friendship.

programme.title: Fomo
programme.desc: Dhruv and Kavya plan a lazy Sunday but feel the pressure of missing out when they see their friends enjoying exciting activities on social media. This leads them to seek ways to spice up their day.

programme.title: Have A Nice Day
programme.desc: A morning rush to work results in a tussle between Dhruv and Kavya, spoiling their moods. Throughout the day, they attempt to make amends, highlighting the irony and sweetness of their relationship.

programme.title: Good Night
programme.desc: A sleepless night due to mosquitoes leads to the discovery that Kavya's ex-boyfriend is getting married, prompting a long conversation about their past relationships.

programme.title: Thank You!
programme.desc: Plans for a dinner at an Iranian restaurant get canceled, leading Dhruv and Kavya to attend a friend's house party. The night evolves into an unexpected adventure, ending at a local fair.

programme.title: Here We Go!
programme.desc: During a visit to a vineyard, Dhruv and Kavya open up about their insecurities and future aspirations, leading to deeper conversations about life and their relationship.

programme.title: Masterji
programme.desc: Kishore, an orphan has only one dream, to join the IndianArmy. When he gets thrown out of his orphanage, Masterji, an ex-army officer who left the army and is now working as a tailor, reluctantly adopts him. The khadoos Masterji and Kishore have only two things in common, their dream to serve the nation and their lonely lives. Will Masterji help Kishore achieve his own unfulfilled dreams to become a soldier, and will Kishore find a father in Masterji?

programme.title: Jaldi Lautna, Captain
programme.desc: Captain Vikrant Sahay and his wife Vibha had a beautiful love story. But Vikrant’s sudden death in battle leaves the otherwise happy-go-lucky Vibha completely shocked. Unable to deal with Vikrant’s death, Vibha decides that the best way to honor him is to fulfill his dream, by joining the army herself, much against her family’s wishes. Will Vibha succeed and make her late husband, her family, and the country proud?

programme.title: Shooter Jawan
programme.desc: Tribhuvan, a dedicated Indian Army Officer comes across a young sweeper Tinku, who is a gifted shooter. Tribhuvan knows Tinku has a rare talent, and wants to enroll him in the shooting academy and then the Indian Army. However, owing to Tinku’s caste, his father is afraid of society’s taunts and is completely against this career choice. Will Tinku have the courage to fight back and rise above ‘log kya kahenge’?

programme.title: Sarhad Ke Baad Bhi
programme.desc: Colonel Balram, a retired Indian Army Officer, returns home after several years of service, only to find himself completely disconnected from his son Aman. While Balram is failing at mending his relationship with Aman, he is also silently struggling with mental health issues, because of his days at war. Will Aman ever understand his father’s sacrifices for the country or will it be too late before he realises just how much his father loves him?

programme.title: Veera
programme.desc: Veera’s martyred father’s last wish was for her to join the Indian armed forces. But Veera is not for it. The bravery and patriotism that the country remembers and celebrates him for, are the same things that took Veera’s father away from her. She’s been carrying this anger and hurt in her heart for too long, until she discovers a hidden letter.

programme.title: Bachelorhood
programme.desc: Viraj and Arjun have been best friends since childhood. While Viraj loves his bachelor life and wants Arjun to move in as his flatmate, Arjun has other plans of ‘settling down’. What happens when this bachelor reveals he wants to be ‘unbachelored?’

programme.title: Kabab Mein Haddi
programme.desc: The gang gets together to celebrate Arjun’s big news, but Viraj is concerned whether the dynamics of the group will change with its new addition. Will Khushi win over the gang and get Viraj's stamp of approval?

programme.title: (un)happy Birthday
programme.desc: Its Arjun’s birthday, and Khushi steals the limelight with her unconventional celebration party. Tensions at the party rise when Viraj confronts Arjun about his change in behaviour since he started dating Khushi. Will Arjun pick pyaar or dosti?

programme.title: Mann Ki Rant
programme.desc: The group tries to resolve tensions between Viraj and Arjun, but they both resist. As Viraj rants to Zara and Kaka about his friendships and relationships, he realises more about his true self.

programme.title: Yaar Ki Shaadi
programme.desc: The wedding day is here! Will Viraj stand by Arjun on the most important day of his life, and will he admit his true feelings to a special someone?

programme.title: Conflict Of Interest
programme.desc: Shagun wants to keep the Filmy Weddings business floating and for that she’s willing to go to any lengths. Even if it means shooting a wedding film for Adhira’s ex-boyfriend – Akhil. Adhira is furious and tries her best to sabotage the entire project. Unfortunately, her one big hurdle is Dad, who has taken quite a fondness for Akhil. Check out the episode and find out what happens next!

programme.title: Internal Politics
programme.desc: Adhira and Shagun are in a disagreement about whom to select for a wedding film promo: a boring aunt from the bride's side or a lively drunk aunt from the groom's family. Things take a turn for the complicated when the parents also weigh in with their respective biases.

programme.title: Poaching The Talent
programme.desc: Vishnu, the Agarwal's househelp cum man friday is desired by the sisters as an office boy to help them sort out their business affairs. The parents are naturally opposed to the idea. Thus begins another standoff in the Agarwal household - and Vishnu's position in the house is at stake. Who will finally lay claim over Vishnu's priceless services?

programme.title: Business Secrets
programme.desc: In a footage from a wedding they just filmed, the sisters find a drunken confession from the bride's best friend. It turns out she is the groom's ex - and he hasn't told the bride to be. Caught in the moral dilemma of choosing to tell the bride or not, the sisters find themselves on other ends of the argument. Meanwhile, it is revealed that Adhira's father might have a similar secret hidden from everyone. Will the sisters do the morally right thing or the financially right thing? And what is the father's bizarre obsession with knowing who the groom's mom is?

programme.title: Breach Of Contract
programme.desc: Chasing a competitive business pitch to work for a coveted fashion firm, the sisters are again at different sides of a dilemma. The commitment to the new opportunity would be for a minimum two years, which Adhira is reluctant to make at the age of 25. Whereas Shagun, having already quit her high paying corporate job to join this business, sees no harm in seizing the opportunity with open arms. As their argument turns to a fight, we are left wondering if this is in fact the fight to end all fights. Will the sisters come to the same page and work for the pitch? Or will Happily Hitched disband for good?

programme.title: Awkward Situations With Mom
programme.desc: It humorously shows how moms unintentionally embarrass their kids by bringing up awkward or personal things. The sketches capture the funny yet relatable moments of such situations.

programme.title: Other People Vs Marwaris
programme.desc: The video humorously compares typical behaviors of other people with the unique habits and quirks of Marwaris. It highlights cultural traits, spending styles, and funny situations that make the contrast entertaining and relatable.

programme.title: Every Curly Haired Girl
programme.desc: The video humorously portrays the struggles and quirks of having curly hair. It highlights the funny situations, compliments, and frustrations curly-haired girls experience in daily life.

programme.title: When Your Best Friend Gets Married
programme.desc: You’re excited, emotional, jealous, and proud all at once—the shift from daily friendship to occasional calls hits harder than expected.

programme.title: Signs You Have The Best Sister
programme.desc: The video shows the sweet and funny moments that prove you have the best sister. It highlights her support, teasing, and unconditional love that make the sibling bond so special.

programme.title: If Indian Moms Were Spies
programme.desc: The video imagines Indian moms with spy-like abilities — covertly listening, gathering info, and being always alert. It highlights the funny extremes of their awareness, intuition, and how nothing gets past them unnoticed.

programme.title: Every Cat Lover
programme.desc: This compilation video humorously contrasts the behaviors and quirks of dog lovers and cat lovers. The "Cat Lovers" segment features Madhu Gudi with her cat Goldy, showcasing typical cat behaviors like seeking attention, sitting on fans, and the unique bond between a cat and its owner

programme.title: When Dadi Is Your Bff
programme.desc: Your grandmother becomes your confidante, partner-in-crime, and emotional support — blurring generational lines. The video shows the fun, loyalty, and occasional friction in that special bond.

programme.title: When You Find The One
programme.desc: That magical, awkward, life-changing moment when you realize your partner might be “the one” — complete with butterflies, hesitation, overthinking, and emotional confession. The video balances realism with romantic idealism.

programme.title: Awkward Conversations With Dad
programme.desc: The “so what did you study?” “When will you settle down?” “Why aren’t you drinking less?” kind of dad-talk — full of pauses, sighs, and “fine, I’m fine”. The video captures the cringe and charm of talks with dad.

programme.title: When You're Expecting A Baby
programme.desc: The mood swings, cravings, excitement, panic, and sweet chaos of expecting parents navigating pregnancy together.

programme.title: Fashion Conversations With Your Sister
programme.desc: Two sisters (or siblings) talk fashion — from borrowing clothes, critiquing style choices, to fighting over accessories. It’s a mix of rivalry, admiration, and shared style secrets.

programme.title: Thoughts You Have While Waxing
programme.desc: The video humorously captures the funny, awkward, and sometimes painful thoughts that run through your mind while waxing. It highlights the relatable mix of dread, discomfort, and self-reflection during the process.

programme.title: Friendship: Then Vs Now
programme.desc: The video compares friendships in school/college days with how they are now. It highlights the nostalgia, changes, and the bond that still remains strong.

programme.title: When Your Bff Has Another Bff
programme.desc: The shift when your best friend starts spending more time with someone else; the strange mix of feeling replaced, missing your old dynamic, trying not to be jealous while still wanting to hang out with them.

programme.title: Certificate Of Nationalism
programme.desc: A satirical take on the idea that love for the country now comes with a “certificate” you must constantly prove.

programme.title: When Your Partner Loves You But Can't Express It
programme.desc: The video portrays relationships where one partner struggles to express love. It highlights the sweet, awkward, and heartfelt moments of unspoken affection.

programme.title: When You Realise Your Younger Sibling Has Grown Up
programme.desc: That surreal shift when your once-tiny sibling starts adulting—and you’re hit by nostalgia and pride.

programme.title: Things Bros Do
programme.desc: The video showcases the funny, quirky, and sometimes annoying things brothers typically do. It highlights the playful teasing, mischief, and unique bond shared between bros.

programme.title: We All Deserve A Boss Like Him
programme.desc: Here’s the boss who actually gets it: acknowledges your effort, cracks a joke when stress hits, listens when you complain—and makes coming to work less of a drag.

programme.title: If 90s Bollywood Romance Happened Today
programme.desc: The video imagines a 90s Bollywood-style romance—and shows how it would play out today with modern technology and social norms. It contrasts the dramatic, over-the-top declarations and music of old with how things like texting, social media, and everyday logistics change the romance vibe.

programme.title: When You Have A Crush On Your Boss Part 2
programme.desc: The series continues with awkward glances, mis-read messages and “Did they really mean that?!” moments as you try to navigate a crush on your boss in the office. All while pretending you’re just “team-meeting focused.”

programme.title: Holi Special: Story Of 4 Single Friends
programme.desc: The video shows four single friends celebrating Holi together. It highlights their fun, playful banter, and how friendship makes festivals special.

programme.title: Reconnecting With Your School Friends
programme.desc: After years apart, meeting school friends again brings nostalgia, awkwardness, comparisons (life, looks, careers), and the joy of remembering old stories. The video captures that mix of “I remember you” and “what happened to you.”

programme.title: Awkward Moments In College
programme.desc: The video humorously captures the everyday awkward situations students face in college. It highlights embarrassing encounters, funny slip-ups, and relatable campus moments.

programme.title: When Arts Student Falls For Science Student
programme.desc: A charming opposites-attract story that highlights the funny clashes between creativity and logic when an arts student dates a science student.

programme.title: Story Of Every Government Exam Student
programme.desc: A young aspirant juggles coaching, hopes and endless revisions while repeating “Next time for sure!” with every attempt. The video captures the roller-coaster of determination, procrastination and exam stress.

programme.title: Things Only Girl Best Friends Do
programme.desc: From matching outfits, inside jokes, sister-level honesty, to secret plans and shared playlists — this video showcases how girl best friends operate in their own world. Full of loyalty, giggles and codewords.

programme.title: When You're Single And Your Friends Are Dating
programme.desc: The video humorously portrays the dynamics when you're single and your friends are in relationships. It highlights the feeling of being the "third wheel" and the humorous situations that arise from it.

programme.title: Indians At A Tailor Shop
programme.desc: The video portrays the typical experiences and interactions one has when visiting a tailor shop in India. It highlights the common scenarios, misunderstandings, and amusing moments that occur during such visits.

programme.title: Struggles Of Having Long Hair
programme.desc: From endless tangles to hair-in-everything problems—a funny look at the pain of long locks.

programme.title: Planning Your First Time
programme.desc: A funny yet honest portrayal of the nervousness, overthinking, and awkward planning that come with preparing for your “first time.”

programme.title: Things Newly Married Couples Do
programme.desc: This video explores the relatable and often humorous experiences of couples after getting married and the honeymoon phase is over. It highlights the changes in dynamics as they transition from being boyfriend/girlfriend to husband and wife.

programme.title: If Doctors Could Hear Your Thoughts
programme.desc: A hilarious peek into what would happen if doctors could hear every anxious, dramatic, or random thought their patients have during check-ups.

programme.title: If Exam Subjects Were People
programme.desc: Math, Science, English, and History as human personalities—some annoying, some fun, and all too relatable.

programme.title: When A Topper Falls In Love With A Backbencher
programme.desc: The academic world collides with romance: one’s always ahead in class, the other’s lagging behind — yet hearts beat anyway. The video shows how opposites in studies find common ground in love.

programme.title: 8 Ways Your Partner Drives You Mad
programme.desc: A humorous checklist of those little habits and quirks in your significant other that drive you crazy — from hogging the blanket to weird sleeping positions. All done in relatably exaggerated sketches.

programme.title: When You Can't Go Home For Diwali
programme.desc: The video highlights the bittersweet experience of not being able to go home for Diwali, featuring a student who misses her family's celebrations. Despite her initial disappointment, her family surprises her by joining her, making her Diwali special

programme.title: Thoughts Babies Have
programme.desc: In a baby’s world: “Why am I small? What’s that giant thing called a toy? Where’s my milk again?” — the video imagines the simple but curious internal tongue of a baby. It’s adorable and funny at the same time.

programme.title: When You Are An Only Child
programme.desc: The perks (undivided attention, full bed) and pangs (no sibling backup, solitude) of being an only child — all wrapped in funny, bittersweet moments. It’s a tribute to a life of solo siblinghood.

programme.title: Every Make-Up Lover Ever
programme.desc: From obsessing over blending brushes to dramatic contour fails—this one’s for every beauty enthusiast.

programme.title: When Your Sibling Moves Away (rakhi Special)
programme.desc: On the occasion of Rakhi, the bittersweet parting: your sibling’s move away triggers both nostalgia and the awkward new dynamic when you’re no longer under the same roof.

programme.title: When Your Partner Cheats On You
programme.desc: The emotional roller-coaster of discovering infidelity: the trust shattered, the shock, the confrontation and the difficult decision of what comes next. Heart-break meets self-reflection.

programme.title: Other Students Vs Me
programme.desc: The video humorously contrasts the habits and achievements of “other students” with your own. It highlights the funny struggles, procrastination, and relatable moments of trying to keep up in school or college.

programme.title: When A Delhi Boy Falls For A Mumbai Girl
programme.desc: Culture-clash, language mishaps, food-fights and the long-distance jokes: a Delhi boy navigating the Mumbai way of life while falling for a girl who lives it.

programme.title: Story Of Every Ca
programme.desc: The journey of a Chartered Accountant aspirant — sleepless nights, endless revisions, social life sacrificed, and pressure from family & society. It portrays CA life as a tough balancing act between dreams and stress.

programme.title: When You Date A Food Lover
programme.desc: Dates revolve around food, arguments over “where to eat,” and the constant temptation to steal bites from your partner’s plate.

programme.title: Other Couples Vs Us
programme.desc: The video humorously contrasts idealized, picture-perfect couples with the messy, realistic dynamics of everyday relationships. It highlights the funny, imperfect moments that make love authentic and relatable.

programme.title: The Son-In-Law You Deserve
programme.desc: A comedic take on the “perfect” damaad every Indian parent dreams of—respectful, helpful, responsible. But real-life sons-in-law rarely fit this mythical description.

programme.title: When You Go Home After A Long Time
programme.desc: The comfort of maa ke haath ka khana, nosy relatives, and the joy of rediscovering your childhood bedroom.

programme.title: Middle Class Things We All Do
programme.desc: A nostalgic, humorous take on all the small, quirky habits that define being middle class in India – from rationing electricity to bargaining at every shop. It highlights the shared pride and compromises of a “middle class” lif

programme.title: When You Have A Crush On Someone
programme.desc: Every overthinker’s paradise: decoding texts, overanalyzing smiles, and mistaking friendliness for love.

programme.title: Every Exam Preparation Ever
programme.desc: This FilterCopy video humorously captures the chaotic and relatable struggles of exam preparation. Ashish Chanchlani and Viraj showcase every student’s panic, procrastination, and last-minute cramming moments before exams.

programme.title: Moving From A Small Town To A Big City
programme.desc: A heartfelt story about chasing dreams, culture shock, loneliness, and finally finding your place in the chaos.

programme.title: If Your Life Were A Rajinikanth Movie
programme.desc: Over-the-top drama, slow-mo entries, and impossible stunts—because life would be so much cooler with Rajini-style swag.

programme.title: School Lunch Breaks
programme.desc: The video captures the chaos and excitement of school lunch breaks. It highlights the rush, the friendships, and the small adventures that make those moments memorable.

programme.title: When Your Partner Is Annoying But Cute
programme.desc: The video shows all the little annoying things partners do that can drive you crazy. Yet, it highlights how those same quirks make them irresistibly cute and lovable.

programme.title: Friend Vs. Best Friend
programme.desc: The difference between a “friend” and a “best friend”—one borrows your charger, the other your whole life.

programme.title: If Office Were Like School (teachers' Day Special)
programme.desc: A fun reversal where bosses become teachers, projects become homework, and office life feels just like school again.

programme.title: Bhai Vs. Bhai
programme.desc: This FilterCopy video captures the playful rivalry and camaraderie between brothers. Ayush Mehra and Rohan Shah bring to life the humorous, competitive, and teasing moments that define a classic “brother vs. brother” dynamic.

programme.title: Every Dog Lover
programme.desc: The video humorously portrays the unique and often over-the-top behaviors of dog lovers. It highlights their deep affection for their canine companions, from treating them like family members to prioritizing their pets over other human relationships, all in a relatable and comical way.

programme.title: If Salary Were A Person
programme.desc: The video imagines salary as a person in everyday life. It humorously portrays the excitement of arrival and the quick disappearance of money.

programme.title: Breaking Up With Parents
programme.desc: A domestic confrontation where a child urgently says “We need to talk!” to their parent, initiating a comical yet tense conversation. It captures the awkwardness and emotional weight of such discussions within a family context.

programme.title: Story Of Every Engineering Student
programme.desc: The video captures the relatable struggles and experiences of every engineering student. It highlights late-night study sessions, project stress, and the funny moments that make college life unforgettable.

programme.title: Things Women Are Tired Of Hearing
programme.desc: The accumulation of “helpful suggestions”, unsolicited advice, outdated compliments and micro-insults that women hear daily. The video uses humour to underline serious patterns of gender expectations.

programme.title: When You Have A Girl Best Friend
programme.desc: A funny take on the quirks of having a girl best friend. Shared secrets and playful banter make the friendship unforgettable.

programme.title: When Your Sister Acts Like Your Mom
programme.desc: The video shows how an older sister can sometimes take on mom-like duties—disciplining, caring, worrying—but also still be someone you laugh with and goof around with. It captures that dual role of caregiver + sibling, showing both the annoying and comforting sides of it.

programme.title: College First Year Vs. Final Year
programme.desc: This video comically illustrates the stark differences in experiences between a student's first year and final year of college. It highlights the evolution of behavior, priorities, and attitudes towards academics, friendships, and overall college life

programme.title: When Your Sister Acts Like Your Mom
programme.desc: The video shows how an older sister can sometimes take on mom-like duties—disciplining, caring, worrying—but also still be someone you laugh with and goof around with. It captures that dual role of caregiver + sibling, showing both the annoying and comforting sides of it.

programme.title: Break Up Ya Patch Up | Filtercopy Talkies | S01e07
programme.desc: A couple stuck between heartbreak and hope tries to decide whether to end things or try again. Emotional nostalgia battles with the fear of repeating old mistakes.

programme.title: Story Of Every Medical Student
programme.desc: A med-student is buried under anatomy books, clinical rounds and family expectations while secretly longing for a normal weekend. It shows the blend of smart-alecs, survival mode and little victories typical in med-college.

programme.title: When You Are The Elder Sibling

programme.desc: The video is a collection of relatable "micro-moments" that define the bond between a father and son. It captures the transition from a relationship based on authority to one based on friendship, albeit with the classic "dad" quirks remaining intact.

programme.title: Every Roadtrip Ever
programme.desc: The video captures the chaotic, funny, and predictable cycle of an Indian road trip with friends. It moves through the high expectations of the planning phase to the messy (but memorable) reality of actually being on the road.

programme.title: Annoying Things People Do In Your Car
programme.desc: The video is a comedic breakdown of the various ways passengers can drive a car owner (the "driver") absolutely crazy. It captures the struggle of trying to keep your car pristine while your friends treat it like a public lounge.

programme.title: Types Of Shoppers
programme.desc: The video humorously identifies and satirizes the various personas we all adopt the moment we step into a shopping center. It breaks down the stereotypes of shopping and, in classic FilterCopy fashion, flips the script on gendered expectations—showing that men can be just as obsessive or indecisive as women when it comes to fashion.

programme.title: Thoughts You Have While Studying
programme.desc: The video is a comedic dive into the brain of a student who is desperately trying to focus but is constantly derailed by distractions, procrastination, and existential dread. It perfectly captures the "pre-exam" struggle where everything in the world seems more interesting than the textbook in front of you.

programme.title: Backbenchers Vs Toppers
